evidence.observation: A separate charged item model gives the weapon a bounded visual state without changing its persistent identity.
The tall sprite sheet keeps the hooked blade and long shaft readable across frames while localized details change.

observations.statement: Named model and texture variants make charged, thrown, or transformed states explicit resource contracts.
Large weapon identity survives animation when the primary blade-to-handle mass relationship stays stable.
Bright state accents are most readable when concentrated around the blade or energy-bearing functional region.

derivedRules.statement: Validate that the outer contour and functional zones identify the equipment archetype at native inventory scale.
Confine accent colors to guards, edges, fasteners, or motifs instead of replacing the dominant material ramp.
Represent gameplay-relevant equipment states with explicit bounded model or texture variants.
